plan.title: persistence-migration: migrate PRISM to a memory-authoritative runtime with synchronous authoritative journals, asynchronous checkpoint/materialization persistence, bounded hot state, and explicit cold-query semantics; finish by reviewing with the user whether a separate persistence worker deserves a follow-up plan.
plan.goal: persistence-migration: migrate PRISM to a memory-authoritative runtime with synchronous authoritative journals, asynchronous checkpoint/materialization persistence, bounded hot state, and explicit cold-query semantics; finish by reviewing with the user whether a separate persistence worker deserves a follow-up plan.

plan.title: persistence-migration: migrate PRISM to a memory-authoritative runtime with synchronous authoritative journals, asynchronous checkpoint/materialization persistence, bounded hot state, and explicit cold-query semantics; finish by reviewing with the user whether a separate persistence worker deserves a follow-up plan.
plan.goal: persistence-migration: migrate PRISM to a memory-authoritative runtime with synchronous authoritative journals, asynchronous checkpoint/materialization persistence, bounded hot state, and explicit cold-query semantics; finish by reviewing with the user whether a separate persistence worker deserves a follow-up plan.

node.title: Specify authoritative-vs-derived persistence boundaries and crash semantics for every persisted domain

acceptance.label: Each persisted domain has an explicit durability class, crash guarantee, source-of-truth owner, rebuild path, and allowed read surfaces.
The migration rules align with docs/PERSISTENCE_STATE_CLASSIFICATION.md and do not leave any snapshot or compatibility view as implicit semantic authority.

node.title: Design backend-neutral persistence interfaces that separate synchronous journal appends from asynchronous checkpoints and materialized views

acceptance.label: The interface shape remains compatible with local SQLite first and future shared-runtime backends.
The store/runtime boundary has explicit interfaces for authoritative journal writes, checkpoint reads/writes, and cold-query backends instead of one snapshot-shaped Store authority.

node.title: Refactor WorkspaceSession, watch, and runtime plumbing so live in-memory state is authoritative and no core request path depends on SqliteStore as the semantic source of truth

acceptance.label: Concrete SqliteStore coupling is reduced enough that a memory-authoritative runtime can evolve without rewriting every request-path consumer.
Core runtime/session/watch code no longer treats SqliteStore as the canonical semantic state holder for live operations.

node.title: Implement synchronous authoritative journals for coordination continuity and other crash-sensitive workflow state

acceptance.label: Plan-close, blocker, and coordination mutation semantics no longer depend on async snapshot persistence for correctness.
Plans, tasks, claims, artifacts, reviews, handoffs, and other policy-relevant workflow continuity survive crashes through a minimal synchronous durable write path.

node.title: Implement synchronous authoritative journals for lineage/history deltas and authored memory and outcome events

acceptance.label: Hydrated snapshots remain accelerators only; the event/journal layer is the semantic recovery authority for these domains.
Rename/lineage continuity, authored memory facts, and authored outcome facts survive crashes through append-oriented durable writes.

node.title: Convert graph snapshots, derived edges, projections, workspace tree state, and compatibility read models into asynchronous coalesced checkpoints and materializations

acceptance.label: Checkpoint and materialization work can coalesce multiple runtime updates into fewer storage writes without changing semantic truth.
Single-file and small-batch runtime writes no longer synchronously pay for rebuildable graph/projection/checkpoint persistence.

node.title: Preserve bounded hot memory and codify explicit hot-only, hot-plus-cold, and cold-backed query paths

acceptance.label: Each query surface explicitly declares whether it reads authoritative hot memory, merges hot memory with cold persisted state, or serves a cold-backed historical view.
The migration does not accidentally hydrate unbounded historical or projection state into RAM.

node.title: Implement checkpoint-plus-journal recovery, revision-lag observability, and replay bounds for restart behavior

acceptance.label: Runtime recovery correctness comes from authoritative journal replay plus bounded checkpoints rather than snapshot authority.
The system exposes replay time, checkpoint age, backlog, and hot-versus-persisted revision lag so recovery and lag are observable.

node.title: Validate crash safety, restart behavior, latency reduction, memory bounds, and multi-instance shared-runtime correctness for the migration

acceptance.label: Tests and live dogfooding cover crash recovery, cold-query correctness, checkpoint lag, reduced hot-path persistence cost, and bounded memory growth.
The migration is documented clearly enough that follow-on persistence changes can preserve the same authority and durability rules.

node.title: Review the migration outcome with the user and decide together whether a separate persistence worker deserves a follow-up plan

acceptance.label: If needed, the follow-up worker plan is scoped from post-migration evidence rather than guessed upfront.
The user and agent explicitly evaluate whether remaining contention, isolation needs, or backend evolution justify a separate persistence-worker follow-up plan.
